days.title: Arrival & Akihabara Exploration
days.rationale: Light post-flight activity in Akihabara suitable for a tired family. The junior zone at SEGA is perfect for a quick, low-stress play session after jet lag.

days.title: Tokyo Character Street & Kanda Soba
days.rationale: Visit Tokyo Character Street for morning shopping followed by lunch at Katsukura Soba in Nihonbashi/Kanda area. Both locations are close to each other and the hotel.

days.title: Kitchenware District Adventure
days.rationale: Dedicated day for Kappabashi. Start with street shopping (ID=61) then attend the food sample workshop (ID=59). Both are in Taito-ku and very close together.

days.title: Ghibli Animation Workshop
days.rationale: Full day trip to Koganei for the Ghibli Museum workshop. This requires significant travel time and advance planning, so it stands alone.

days.title: Ikebukuro Arcade Fun
days.rationale: Dedicated afternoon at Taito Station Ikebukuro for gentle arcade play suitable for a 4-year-old. Located in Toshima-ku, easy transit from hotel.

days.title: Meguro Ramen Experience
days.rationale: Lunch trip to Meguro for the famous Jiro ramen. The bold flavors are an adventure for older kids/adults while parents watch. Short transit from hotel.

days.title: Flexible Buffer Day
days.rationale: No specific stops assigned to allow for rest or revisiting favorite spots. The hotel (ID=70) serves as the base.

days.title: Airport Transfer Preparation
days.rationale: Final day before return flight (assuming flight is on Day 9 or later). Stay near hotel for packing and rest. Hotel ID=70 used as base.

optimization_options.label: Cultural Flow
optimization_options.description: Group by theme — temples one day, food districts another

optimization_options.label: Balanced Pace
optimization_options.description: Mix of walking and transit, varied activities each day

stops.note: Arrive at NRT by 2 PM; take Narita Express to Akihabara (approx 60 mins). Shop/Play after arrival.
Lunch at Katsukura Soba; arrive before 2 PM to avoid waits. No reservation needed.
Tokyo Character Street nearby; explore character goods and souvenirs.
Kappabashi Street open weekdays until 5:30 PM; explore kitchenware and food samples.
Food Sample Workshop (weekends only; verify date). Advance booking required.
Ghibli Museum Workshop requires advance ticket purchase (months ahead). Arrive early for check-in.
Taito Station Ikebukuro open until 10 PM; relaxed afternoon gaming session.
Ramen Jiro Meguro open until 9 PM; expect long lines and no reservations.
Pack luggage and prepare for Narita Express to NRT (depart by 1 PM for evening flight).

cards.title: Katsukura Soba
cards.description: Savor hand-pulled soba noodles in a traditional setting that delights children with their visual appeal. The shop offers kid-friendly portions and mild broths perfect for young palates.
cards.timing: Lunch or dinner; arrive before 2 PM to avoid long waits; no reservation needed

cards.title: Kappabashi Street Food Sample Workshop
cards.description: Participate in a hands-on workshop where you can create your own realistic plastic food models using wax and resin techniques. This unique activity lets children see how the famous fake food displays are actually made while making a souvenir to take home.
cards.timing: Weekends 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; advance booking recommended; 2 hours

cards.title: Ghibli Museum Workshop: Animation Basics
cards.description: Join a specialized animation workshop where children can draw characters and learn the fundamentals of stop-motion techniques. The interactive nature of this class brings the beloved studio's magic to life in an educational setting.
cards.timing: Daily 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM; tickets sold out months in advance
cards.address: 1-6-19 Koganei, Musashino-shi, Tokyo (Note: Requires advance ticket purchase)

cards.title: Kappabashi Street
cards.description: Wander through the world's largest district for kitchenware where you can find everything from realistic wax food samples to professional-grade utensils. The street is lined with over 170 shops offering a fascinating glimpse into Japanese culinary culture.

cards.title: Tokyo Character Street
cards.description: Discover the ultimate destination for anime and manga merchandise in this multi-level shopping complex filled with beloved characters. Children will be captivated by the endless array of toys, figures, and accessories from their favorite shows.
cards.timing: Open daily 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM; spend 2 hours

cards.title: Ramen Jiro Meguro
cards.description: Experience massive portions of garlic-infused ramen in a no-frills setting that locals love. The bold flavors and unique serving style create an exciting dining experience for adventurous families.
cards.timing: Open from 11:00 AM to 9:00 PM; expect a 70-minute visit.

cards.title: MIMARU TOKYO STATION EAST
cards.description: MIMARU Tokyo Station East offers spacious apartment-style rooms with full kitchens and laundry facilities, making it a practical home base near Ginza Six for families or longer stays. Its prime location on the second floor of a building in Hatchōbori provides easy access to the Shinkansen and major business districts while allowing guests to cook their own meals.
cards.address: Japan, 〒104-0032 Tokyo, Chuo City, Hatchōbori, 1-chōme−12−８ ＥＡＳＴ２階

cards.title: SEGA Akihabara Building No. 3
cards.description: This massive multi-story arcade features a dedicated junior zone with coin-operated cars and gentle racing games perfect for toddlers. Visitors can enjoy classic claw machines and rhythm games while parents relax in the viewing lounge.
cards.timing: Open daily from 9:30 AM to 11:00 PM; allow 2-3 hours for play without booking.

cards.title: Taito Station Ikebukuro
cards.description: Known for its extensive collection of kiddie rides and gentle claw machines, this location is ideal for a four-year-old's first arcade visit. The spacious layout reduces crowding, ensuring a safe play area for families.
cards.timing: Open daily from 9:00 AM to 10:00 PM; plan for a relaxed 2-hour afternoon session.
